reforms.description: Norway has initiated a series of policy actions aimed at improving the efficiency and effectiveness of its education system, including the introduction of a new curriculum and measures to enhance teacher competencies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and teacher competencies is expected to enhance the skill level of the workforce, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy actions have been put into effect as part of the ongoing education reform.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of the education system, which is crucial for long-term human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: The Knowledge Promotion reform launched in 2006 aimed at improving educational outcomes through a new curriculum and enhanced teacher competencies.

reforms.description: The Norwegian government, in collaboration with Statoil, is establishing an experimental carbon capture and storage (CCS) facility at the Mongstad refinery to test different technologies for CO2 separation from post-combustion gases.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in CCS technology is expected to contribute to environmental sustainability and innovation in energy sectors, supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been publicly announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a CCS facility represents a significant investment in research infrastructure aimed at addressing climate change.
reforms.source_quote: The state, together with the Norwegian oil company Statoil, is going to build an experimental complete carbon capture system for the Mongstad refinery.
